title: Inspect the available repo evidence and recommend the best delivery artifact format for 10 x-auto draft posts that need to be reused in two contexts: (1) x-auto queue/runtime compatibility, (2) manual or scripted Notion row insertion. Consider x-auto-health.py field expectations and x-auto contract...
summary_text: Use a **single JSON file containing a top-level array of 10 objects** (the exact shape x-auto-health expects for `post_queue.json`), and include Notion-aligned fields in the same objects so one artifact can seed both pipeline queue and row insertion. Recommended format: **`JSON array`** (not CSV/NDJSON), with object keys: 1. `title` 2. `status` 3. `posted` 4. `scheduled_for` 5. `source_urls` 6. `body` 7. `body_ja` 8. `image_path` 9. `reply_url` 10. `tweet_id` 11. `pillar` 12. `category` 13. `pattern` 14. *(optional but useful)* `notion_page_id` Exact compatibility mapping: - Runtime health checks in `scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py` require the queue items to be parsable objects with `status`, `posted`, and `scheduled_for` fields; missing `scheduled_for` only escalates to fail when `status=="approved``, and date-only `scheduled_for` triggers warning/skip risk. - Contract says Notion is the source-of-truth hub for `Body`, `Body JA`, `Source URLs`, `Image`, `Status`, and `Tweet ID`, so keep `body`, `body_ja`, `source_urls`, `image_path` (runtime), and `tweet_id` in each record. - Keep `reply_url` as explicit `null` unless quote/reply routing demands a value now (current contract note says no separate default thread URL exists for note-link rows). If you want strict contract alignment, also keep `body`/`body_ja` semantically mapped 1:1 to Notion `Body` / `Body JA`, and `source_urls` as the array backing `Source URLs`.
